mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment below 4% tightens labor market, lifting wages and consumer spending, which lifts corporate earnings and thus S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE below 4% is followed by positive 20-day returns in less than 55% of such instances over any two-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, exceeds a high threshold (e.g., an index level of 280), it forces the Fed to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL above 280 is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if Fed funds rate does not increase within 6 months, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous very low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) and very high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 280) signals an overheated economy where the Fed must aggressively tighten, crushing equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the correlation between the interaction term and returns is not statistically negative (p<0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 2%), a rising industrial production index (INDPRO) signals genuine demand growth and is rewarded by equity markets; direction is positive at this point.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases by more than 0.5% month-on-month produce negative 20-day returns in more than 30% of instances, or the regime switch to FEDFUNDS > 2% reverses the sign, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST below 1,500 thousand units) signals a housing market contraction that spills over to consumer confidence and economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: If HOUST below 1500 is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return after such events is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) are elevated and rising together, it signals demand-pull inflation and aggressive Fed tightening, which hurts equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the interaction coefficient in a linear regression is not negative at 5% significance, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL > 280), an increase in the federal funds rate is perceived as reactive tightening that confirms economic overheating, thus driving stock prices down over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a 25bp increase in FEDFUNDS during high inflation leads to positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average excess return is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of retail sales (RSAFS above 650,000) indicates resilient consumer demand that drives corporate revenues and profits, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS above 650,000 is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of quarters, or if the rank IC over rolling 3-year windows is below 0.02, reject.
